original: Abbott Dauferio/Desiderius becomes Pope Victor III
Malcolm IV becomes King of Scots
The Fifth Crusade leaves Acre for Egypt
Magnus Ladulås is crowned King of Sweden in Uppsala Cathedral
Hanzesteden signs peace treaty with Danish king Waldemar IV
Imposter Lambert Simnel crowned as King Edward VI in Dublin
Nomenclator of Leiden University Library appears, the first printed catalog of an institutional library
The Protestant Union is formally dissolved
English kingCharles IIvisits Netherlands
French troops attack into Southern Netherlands
English Parliament guarantees freedom of religion for Protestants
English KingWilliam IIItravels through northern Europe
Irish Rebellion of 1798 led by the United Irishmen against British rule begins
English surveyor George Evans is the first European to discover the Lachlan River, Australia
Pope Leo XII proclaims a Universal Jubilee in an attempt to strengthen a closer bond between the Pope and the Christian people
Pope Pius VIII issues his program for pontificate
"Mary Had A Little Lamb" by Sarah Josepha Hale is first published by Boston firm Marsh, Capen & Lyon
First regular passenger rail service on the Baltimore and Ohio Railroad line features horse-drawn railcars connecting Baltimore to Ellicott's Mills
The First Kingdom of Greece is declared in the London Conference
Escaped slave Anthony Burns is arrested by US Deputy marshals in Boston under the Fugitive Slave Act
Alexandria, Virginia, occupied by Union troops
Beardslee field telegraph used for 1st time
Berkeley, California named (for George Berkeley, Bishop of Cloyne)
Memoria of Jackson Kemper, 1st Missionary Bishop in US
Leo Delibes' opera "Le Roi l'a Dit" ("The King Has Spoken") premieres at the Opéra-Comique in Paris
CA Parker (Harvard) wins 1st American bike race at Beacon Park in Boston
Overloaded Canadian river ferry "Princess Victoria" sinks near London, Ontario, 180 die
Turkey cedes Thessaly and Arta back to Greece.
Anti-Monopoly and the Greenback parties unite to form the People's Party in the US
Sultan Bargash of Zanzibar grants E African Association at East African harbors
Geo Train & Sam Wall circle world in record 67 days, Tacoma-Tacoma
Tivoli Theater of Varieties opens in London
Henry Irving becomes the first actor to receive a knighthood
1st auto repair shop opens (Boston)
Seventy-eight miners die in the Caerphilly pit disaster in South Wales
Cleve's Bill Bradley is 1st ALer to hit a HR run in 4 consecutive games, not duplicated until Babe Ruth does it June 25, 1918
Empire Day 1st celebrated in Britain
Belgium Catholic socialist/liberal parliamentary election
John Masefields "Tragedy of Nan" premieres in London
Bristol University granted Royal Charter
Conscription begins in Britain
French driven out of Fort Douaumont after 500 killed or injured
Last British-Indian contract workers arrive in Suriname
US pilot William Thaw shoots down a German Fokker
British officer General Poole lands at Murmansk, the Russian port on the Barents Sea
Cleveland starter Stan Coveleski sets club record for most innings pitched (19) in a complete game as the Indians beat the New York Yankees, 3-2 at the Polo Grounds, NYC

Bulhoek Massacre: police commissioner Colonel Theodore Truter leads 6 squadrons and artillery detachment against Israelite religious sect collected at annual gathering on land of leader Enoch Mgijima at Ntabalanga; 190 killed
First Parliament for Northern Ireland elected
The first Comrades Marathon takes place in Durban, South Africa, created by WWI veteran Vic Clapham to commemorate the South African soldiers killed during the war
Record temperature in Netherlands for May recorded: 35.6°C (96°F)
Russian-Italian trade agreement signed
Canada grants women the right to vote in federal elections, though First Nations women still unable to without giving up their status
Italian aviator Umberto Nobile flies airship Italia over North Pole again (crashes onto ice pack a day later)
Chicago WS pitcher Ted Lyons and Detroit's George Uhle go 21 innings before Tigers get a run to win, 6-5; longest game (3 hours, 31 mins) ever at Comiskey Park, Chicago
1st air-conditioned train installed-B&O Railroad
Colombia and Peru sign an accord regarding the river city of Leticia in the Amazon
First Major League night baseball game, Reds beat Philadelphia 2-1 in Cincinnati
Dutch bishops forbid membership of Nazi party
Tony Lazerri 2 grand slams (11 RBIs); Ben Chapman sets record by reaching 1st 7 times safely, Yanks beat A's 25-2
1st night game at St Louis Sportsman Park (Indians 3, Browns 2)
Dutch army demobilizes
German tanks reach Arras, France
NY Giants swat Boston Bees, 8-1, in 1st night game at Polo Grounds, NYC
German athlete Rudolf Harbig runs world record 1,000m in 2:21.5 at Dresden, Germany
German battleship Bismarck sinks British battlecruiser HMS Hood; 1,416 die, 3 survive
U-441 shoots Sunderland seaplane down over Gulf of Biskaje
Icelandic voters severe all ties with Denmark
Racial segregation in Washington, D.C. restaurants ruled illegal
US performs nuclear test at Enewetak Atoll (atmospheric tests)
Dr Peter Murray Marshall becomes 1st African American to head an American Medical Association unit (New York County)
First rocket to reach 150 miles (241 km) in altitude blasts off from White Sands, New Mexico
German airline Lufthansa forms
IBM announces vacuum tube "electronic" brain that could perform 10 million operations an hour
1st Eurovision Song Contest: Lys Assia for Switzerland wins singing "Refrain" in Lugano
Conclusion of the Sixth Buddhist Council on Vesak Day, marking the 2,500 year anniversary after the Lord Buddha's Parinibbāna
Anti-American riots breakout in Taipei, Taiwan
Heavy earthquake strikes Colombia
United Press Association and International News Service merge to form United Press International
Empire Day renamed Commonwealth Day in Great Britain
First house with built-in bomb shelter exhibited (Pleasant Hills, Pennsylvania)
One millionth Dutch telephone installed
27 Freedom Riders arrested in Jackson, Mississippi
Cyprus joins the Council of Europe.
NASA Explorer Ionosphere research mission fails to reach Earth orbit
"Hud", directed by Martin Ritt and starring Paul Newman, premieres in Hollywood, California
1st Lockheed A-12 to crash, CIA pilot Ken Collins ejects safely
18th Tony Awards: "Luther" (play) & "Hello, Dolly!" (musical) win
Panic in Lima Peru soccer stadium, kills 300
Supreme Court declares federal law allowing post office to intercept communist propaganda is unconstitutional
AFL grants a franchise to Cincinnati Bengals
French film "Belle de Jour" is released, directed by Luis Buñuel and starring Catherine Deneuve
American boxer Bob Foster defeats holder Dick Tiger for the world light-heavyweight Championship at Madison Square Gardens, New York (goes on to defend title x 14)
FLQ separatists bomb the U.S. consulate in Quebec City
Haiti closes down shortwave station 4VEH for 40 days
"Sugar, Sugar" single released by cartoon band The Archies (Billboard Song of the Year, 1969)
The Beatles' "Get Back" single goes #1 and stays #1 for 5 weeks
British guitarist Peter Green quits Fleetwood Mac to join a religious cult
The drilling of the Kola Superdeep Borehole begins in the Soviet Union
A commuter bus plunges into Panama Canal, killing 38 of 43 aboard
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
George [Earl] Jellicoe resigns as British Lord Privy Seal and Leader of the House of Lords
Dutch government of De Uyl decides to obtain an F-16
Soviet spacecraft Soyuz 18 launches from the Baikonur Cosmodrome, destined for the Salyut 4 space station
1st commercial SST flight to North America (Concorde to Washington, D.C.)
In the Judgment of Paris, wine testers rate wines from California higher than their French counterparts, challenging the notion of France being the foremost producer of the world's best wines
USSR President Podgorny resigns
American management consultant Marilyn Loden first coins the term "glass ceiling" to describe invisible career barriers for women
Dutch Investment bill (WIR) law goes into effect
32nd Cannes Film Festival: "Apocalypse Now" directed byFrancis Ford Coppolaand "Die Biechtrommel" directed by Volker Schlondorff jointly awarded the Palme d'Or
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
"Rock Lobster" single by B-52s hits #56
Iran rejects a call to World Court to release US hostages
Stanley Cup Final, Nassau Coliseum, Uniondale, NY: In first Finals appearance, NY Islanders beat Philadelphia Flyers, 5-4 in OT for a 4 games to 2 series win
Hostage situation ends at Central Bank in Barcelona, Spain
Liberation of Khorramshahr; Iranians recapture of the port city of Khorramshahr from the Iraqis during the Iran-Iraq War
Fred Sinowatz succeeds Bruno Kreisky as Chancellor of Austria
Supreme Court rules government can deny tax breaks to schools that racially discriminated against students
Det Tigers win AL record 17th straight road game
Reginald Huffstetler treads water for 985 hrs
Stanley Cup Final, Saddledome, Calgary, Alberta: Montreal Canadiens beat Calgary Flames, 4-3 for 4-1 series victory
Golden Gate Bridge 50th anniversary: Over 800K people show up, 300K walk on bridge at same time, span temporarily flattens from weight (San Francisco, California)
Britain's Parliament passes Section 28 as law prohibiting the promotion of homosexuality. Repealed in 2001/2004.
John Moschitta set record for fast talking: 586 words per minute
Power outage in Boston Garden in NHL's Stanley Cup finals
French Nazi war criminalPaul Touvierarrested at the Society of Saint Pius X monastery in Nice
NY Yankee hurler Lee Gutterman sets record of pitching 30-2/3 innings before giving up his 1st run of season
Sonia Sutcliffe, wife of the Yorkshire Ripper, is awarded £600,000 in damages after winning a libel action against satirical magazine Private Eye (later reduced to £60,000 on appeal).
A car carrying American Earth First! activists Judi Bari and Darryl Cherney explodes in Oakland, California, critically injuring both
Stanley Cup Final, Boston Garden, Boston, MA: Edmonton Oilers beat Boston Bruins, 4-1 for a 4-1 series win; Oilers' 5th Cup win in 7 years
Despite trailing 7-1, NY Yanks tie Milwaukee Brewers & then score 1 in 9th to avoid 5th straight extra inning game
Thai Prime Minister Suchinda Kraprayoon resigns after 48 days in office
Eritrea achieved independence from Ethiopia after 30-year civil war
Kurd rebellion kills 33 soldiers and 5 citizens in Turkey
"Poison" singer Bret Michaels is involved in a car crash
"Spy Hard" starring Leslie Nielsen is released
STS 84 (Atlantis 19), lands
Telstar-5 Proton Launch, Successful
51st Cannes Film Festival: "Mia aioniotita kai mia mera / Eternity and a Day" by Theo Angelopoulos wins the Palme d'Or
Venezuela enters the Antarctic Treaty System.
Israeli troops withdraw from southern Lebanon after 22 years of occupation.
Democrats gain control of the US Senate for the first time since 1994 when Senator James Jeffords of Vermont abandons the Republican Party and declares himself an independent
Fifteen-year-old Sherpa Temba Tsheri becomes the youngest person to reach the summit of Mount Everest
The Versailles wedding hall disaster in Jerusalem, Israel, kills 23 and injures over 200 in Israel's worst-ever civil disaster.
Russia and the United States sign the Moscow Treaty.
Super Rugby Final, Eden Park, Auckland: Auckland Blues beat defending champion Canterbury Crusaders, 21-17 for their 3rd title
French KingPhilip IVoccupies Flanders and captures the Count of Flanders, Guy of Dampierre
John Wesleyis converted, launching the Methodist movement, celebrated annually by Methodists as Aldersgate Day
SmugglerLouis Mandrinconsidered the French Robin Hood is sentenced to be broken on the wheel, a medieval form of torture and execution that breaks the bones of the subject
Samuel Adamswrites instructions for Boston Town Meeting opposing the Sugar Act, laying groundwork for colonial resistance to taxation without representation
John Hancockis unanimously elected President of the Continental Congress
Ludwig van Beethovenand black violinist George Bridgewater perform a concert together at the Augarten, Vienna
GeneralAndrew Jacksoncaptures Pensacola, Florida
Battle of Pichincha,Simón Bolívarsecures the independence of Ecuador from Spain
Samuel Morsetaps out "What hath God wrought" in the world's first telegraph message

German explorerLudwig Leichhardtis awarded the Patron's Medal by the Royal Geographical Society, London in recognition of 'the increased knowledge of the great continent of Australia' gained by his Moreton Bay-Port Essington journey
Pottawatomie Massacre:John Brownand abolitionist settlers kill five pro-slavery settlers in Franklin County, Kansas
Union Major GeneralBenjamin Butlerdeclares escaped slaves "contraband of war", after three slaves escaped to Fort Monroe - will become Union policy and change the course of the war
Alexandra Palace opens onQueen Victoria's 54th birthday with a grand celebration including concerts, recitals and fireworks
Leo von Caprivi succeedsOtto von Bismarckas Chancellor of Germany
Lowell Observatory, Arizona, first begins observations of Mars with an eighteen-inch telescope, leads its builderPercival Lowellto conclude there are canals on Mars
Thomas Edisoninvents telescribe to record telephone conversations
Stanley Baldwinsucceeds AndrewBonar Lawas British Prime Minister
Finnish runnerPaavo Nurmisets world 3000m record (8:25.4) in Berlin, Germany
Record 12 future Baseball Hall of Famers take the field as NY Yankees beat Philadelphia A's, 9-7 at Shibe Park; managers Miller Huggins &Connie Mack, umpire Tom Connolly also HOF'ers
Amy Johnsonbecomes the 1st woman to fly solo from United Kingdom to Australia
NY Yankees' legendary sluggerBabe Ruthhomers in both games of a doubleheader (beat Philadelphia A's 10-6 & 11-1); gives him 9 HR's in one week
Dmitri Shostakovich's Preludes premieres in Moscow
Adolf Hitlerand General von Rundstedt issue a Halt Order stopping German armed divisions and allowing British and French armies to evacuate through Calais and Dunkirk
DutchQueen Wilhelminaspeaks on BBC radio
Final entry in the Stroop Report, detailing the destruction of the Jewish Warsaw Ghetto, compiled by Nazi officers, later used as evidence in the Nuremberg trials
German Admiral Donitz stops U-boat operations in the Atlantic Ocean - turning point in the Battle of the Atlantic
Enver Hoxhabecomes head of Albania's anti fascists
Bill Dickey replacesJoe McCarthyas New York Yankees manager
Benjamin Britten's "Beggar's Opera" premieres at Arts Theatre, Cambridge, England
PopePius XIIpublishes encyclical Doctor Mellifluus
Cuban PresidentFulgencio Batistaopens military offensive againstFidel Castro's armed rebellion
The Beatles record "Sweet Georgia Brown" and "Swanee" in Hamburg, Germany to close out their contract with producer Bert Kaempfert;Tony Sheridanlater dubs vocal over tracks
US astronautScott Carpenteraboard Aurora 7 (Mercury-Atlas 7) orbits the Earth three times in a flight just under five hours
Barbra Streisandperforms "Happy Days Are Here Again" at White House Press Correspondents Dinner, and meets US PresidentJohn F. Kennedy
Longest HR (471') in Baltimore Memorial Stadium (Harmon Killebrew, Minnesota Twins)
French PresidentCharles De Gaulleproposes referendum & students set fire to Paris bourse
Mick Jaggerand Marianne Faithfull arrested for drug possession in England
"TheDean MartinShow" last airs on NBC-TV
27th Cannes Film Festival: "The Conversation" directed byFrancis Ford Coppolawins the Grand Prix du Festival International du Film

RCA releases "Diamond Dogs",David Bowie's 8th studio album, recorded in London and the Netherlands, with cover design by artist Guy Peellaert, it peaks in the U.S. charts at No. 5, and goes to No. 1 in the U.K. and Canada
Muhammad AliTKOs Richard Dunn in 5 for heavyweight boxing title in Munich
Billy Martinissues a public apology to Reno sportswriter Ray Hagar
"Kagemusha" directed byAkira Kurosawawins the Palme d'Or at the Cannes Film Festival
Margaret Thatcherbecomes the first British Prime Minister to visit Israel
"Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade", directed bySteven Spielberg, starringHarrison Ford, and produced byGeorge Lucas' Lucasfilms premieres
NHL's NY Rangers fire GM & coachPhil Esposito
Weird Al Yankovicrecords three tracks for his UHF movie soundtrack
Kim Basingerfiles for bankruptcy to avoid paying $7.4M settlement
Star Trek episode "Second Chances" airs, guest-starringMae Jemison, the first real-life astronaut to appear on the show
ActorTim Allenarrested for drunk driving in Michigan
Paul McCartneyperforms in his first-ever concert in Russia, in Moscow's Red Square, to a crowd of over 100,000 people
Westminster Bridge across The Thames in London opens, becoming the second such bridge after an earlier bridge fell into decay
Brooklyn Bridge is opened by PresidentChester A. Arthurand NY Governor Grover Cleveland
The Beatles' 4th appearance on "TheEd SullivanShow", features an interview and pre-recorded performance of "You Can't Do That"
